content: We can refactor Memory Networks' explicit external memory storage architecture to solve the context window and memory bloat bottlenecks that plague Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) implementations in large-scale LLM autonomous agents, by dynamically evicting low-value, uncorrelated past state embeddings from Memory Networks' memory bank to feed ICM prediction heads. This lets large-scale autonomous agents retain the curiosity-driven exploration benefits of ICM without exhausting GPU memory from storing thousands of past trajectory embeddings.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
